title: Prompt Injection at Work: Real-World Risks
modules: Beyond the Chatbot: Where Prompt Injection Hides in Everyday Professional Tools
Attack Scenarios: Email Assistants, Document Summarizers, CRM Copilots, and HR Bots
How Injected Instructions Exfiltrate Data, Manipulate Outputs, and Escalate Privileges
Recognizing Suspicious AI Behavior: Warning Signs Any User Can Spot
Organizational Controls: Prompt Hardening, Output Filtering, and Tool Permissions
Building an Incident Response Playbook for AI-Assisted Workflow Compromises
Training Your Workforce: Practical Exercises for Security and Non-Security Staff
synopsis: Prompt injection is no longer a theoretical research curiosity — it is actively exploited through AI-assisted email, document processing, and enterprise copilot tools that millions of professionals use every day, often without any awareness that the threat exists. This course translates the attack mechanics into concrete workplace scenarios and gives both everyday users and security teams the language and actions they need to recognize, report, and reduce exposure.
rationale: Enterprise AI tool adoption has far outpaced workforce awareness of prompt injection risk, creating a wide-open social engineering surface that existing security awareness programs almost universally fail to address.
learning_outcome: Learners can identify prompt injection risk in the AI-assisted tools they use daily, apply basic input and output hygiene practices, and contribute to or implement an organizational response plan when AI tool behavior appears manipulated.
source_signals: Anti-recruiter prompt injection attack in LinkedIn profile
AI-powered Bing Chat spills its secrets via prompt injection attack
